_comment: The events registry. Four recurrence kinds:
annual      — same month/day every year (Ferragosto, 15 Aug).
nth_weekday — 'first Sunday of May'. weekday 0=Sun..6=Sat; nth 1..5, or -1 for last.
computed    — derived from a rule (Easter computus; Carnevale = Easter - 47).
irregular   — may not happen in a given year at all. Occurrences listed explicitly.
An entry with requiresEvent:true is DORMANT in a year with no
occurrence; it does NOT fall back into rotation. A World Cup idiom
A quadrennial event in this registry is just an override written four years early,
with a window instead of a date. Same work, done earlier, where forgetting is
impossible. Milano-Cortina 2026 was in ITALY and needed four lines written any time
in the last six years. It passed in February. That is the argument for the registry.

mx_independence.name: Independencia de México
mx_independence.note: El Grito is the night of the 15th. NOT Cinco de Mayo — do not let an entry drift there.

ferragosto.name: Ferragosto
ferragosto.note: Italy closes. Rich material: ponte, chiuso per ferie, esodo.

world_cup.name: FIFA World Cup
world_cup.note: 2026: USA/Canada/Mexico. 2030: Spain/Portugal/Morocco — dates not yet published, hence null. A null occurrence means KNOWN EVENT, UNKNOWN DATES: entries stay dormant and the validator warns, rather than guessing.

olympics.name: Olympic Games
olympics.note: 2026 Milano-Cortina (ITALY — the most on-brand event this product could have had). 2028 Los Angeles. 2030 French Alps, dates unpublished → null = known event, unknown dates.

madre_mx.name: Día de la Madre (México)
madre_mx.note: Mexico fixes it to the 10th regardless of weekday. Spain and Italy do not.
